fault_hypothesis: Lines 76-77 incorrectly pop the last element when both lists share the same last element, but the merge already correctly includes both copies (one from each list). This post-processing removal is wrong and causes the last shared element to be dropped.

constraints.intent: After the merge loop and extend calls, merged must contain exactly len(left)+len(right) elements before any pop.
After popping, the merged list should still have len(left)+len(right) elements, but the pop incorrectly removes one.
At each loop iteration end, the number of elements appended equals i+j (the total indices advanced).

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1]) or len(merged__1) > len(left) + len(right) - 1

constraints.intent: The condition triggering the pop should never be true for a correctly merged list where both last elements are already included.
